steps.title: Spot the pattern of repetition
steps.content: The passage uses relentless repetition of sameness language: “like one another,” “people equally like one another,” “same hours,” “same sound,” “same work,” “every day was the same,” “every year the counterpart.” This repetitive emphasis on sameness applies to both the town's physical layout and its inhabitants, making uniformity the passage's defining focus.

steps.title: Match the signal to the choices
steps.content: Choice A directly names the passage's main purpose: emphasizing the uniformity of both the town and its people. The other options either misidentify what the passage emphasizes (B on limited opportunities, D on pleasantness) or seize on a secondary detail rather than the main point (C on losing track of time). This matches choice A — A is correct.
